headline: Apollo Private Credit Fund Limits Withdrawals After Requests Exceed 5% Cap
summary: An Apollo Global Management private credit fund capped withdrawals at its stated 5% limit after investors asked to redeem more than double that amount.  The Apollo Debt Solutions BDC, a type of semiliquid private credit fund marketed to wealthy individuals, received requests to redeem 11.2% of its shares ahead of the most recent quarterly deadline, according to a regulatory filing Monday.  John Zito, co-president of the firm's asset-management arm, has said he generally supports sticking to the 5% limit, The Wall Street Journal reported last week.

headline: Apollo gives investors only 45% of requested withdrawals from $15 billion private credit fund
summary: The withdrawals show that Apollo didn't avoid the rush of investor redemptions plaguing rivals, driven by concern over private credit loans to software firms.

headline: A Look At Apollo Global Management’s Valuation After The Realty Income Joint Venture And New UK Credit Fund
summary: How the Realty Income joint venture reshapes Apollo’s private capital profile The new joint venture with Realty Income centers on approximately 500 US single tenant retail properties on long term net leases, giving Apollo funds exposure to contractual rental cash flows backed by Realty Income’s asset management platform. Apollo Global Management (APO) is committing US$1.0b for a 49% interest in the vehicle. Realty Income retains operational control and a call option from year 7 to year 15,...

headline: Berkshire Hathaway to Invest $1.8 Billion in Tokio Marine
summary: National Indemnity Company, a subsidiary of Berkshire, will make a 2.49% strategic investment in Tokio Marine, according to a statement Monday.  The two companies will collaborate on reinsurance and global investments including mergers and acquisitions.  The move underscores Berkshire’s growing ambitions in Japan, where around six years ago — under the leadership of Warren Buffett — it revealed it had invested in the country’s largest trading houses.
